writing_style.sentence_length: [DESCRIBE SPOKEN SENTENCE LENGTH — e.g. 'very short punchy hook lines followed by slightly longer explanatory sentences . nothing over 20 words per spoken sentence' OR 'consistently short and clipped throughout . sounds like thinking out loud' OR 'varied — short for hooks and emphasis, medium for explanation, never long']
writing_style.paragraph_length_as_beats: [DESCRIBE BEAT LENGTH — e.g. '1–3 spoken sentences per beat . each beat lands one idea before moving on' OR '2–4 sentences per beat . moderate pacing' OR '1 sentence max per beat . aggressive jump cuts']
writing_style.use_of_questions: [true OR false — does the creator ask the viewer rhetorical or direct questions in Reels?]
writing_style.use_of_lists: [true OR false — does the creator verbally list items inside Reels? e.g. 'three things I changed']
writing_style.use_of_emojis_on_screen: [DESCRIBE — e.g. 'never used in on-screen text' OR 'rare . used as visual bullets only' OR 'frequently used in on-screen text to add energy']
writing_style.on_screen_text_style: [DESCRIBE — e.g. 'bold key phrase only . 1–3 words . appears for 1.5–3 seconds . never transcribes spoken words . names the mechanism or number' OR 'full subtitle style . every word on screen']
writing_style.use_of_capitalization_on_screen: [true OR false — e.g. does the creator use ALL CAPS for emphasis in on-screen text?]
writing_style.storytelling_arc: [DESCRIBE NARRATIVE STRUCTURE for Reels — e.g. 'hook (bold claim or challenge) → tension (what most people do wrong) → reframe (what actually works) → proof (one example or result) → CTA (low-friction keyword trigger)' OR 'problem → stakes → mechanism → outcome → CTA' OR 'story open → universal lesson → practical system → CTA']
writing_style.pacing_notes: [DESCRIBE EDITING PACE — e.g. 'jump cut every 3–5 seconds on talking head . B-roll cut timed to spoken emphasis . no transitions . music: lo-fi ambient or none' OR 'slower deliberate pace . fewer cuts . more sustained eye contact with camera']
